}

#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>

struct DummyValidatePolicy {};
struct DummyRefCountPolicy {};

template<size_t element_count = 10>
void demonstrate_block_allocator_behavior() {
    hexi::block_allocator<int, element_count, DummyValidatePolicy, DummyRefCountPolicy> allocator;

    std::cout << "Demonstrating block_allocator with " << element_count << " elements." << std::endl;

    std::vector<int*> allocated_blocks;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count; ++i) {
        int* p = allocator.allocate();
        allocated_blocks.push_back(p);
        std::cout << "Allocated block " << i+1 << " of " << element_count << std::endl;
    }

    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count; ++i) {
        allocator.deallocate(allocated_blocks[i]);
        std::cout << "Deallocated block " << i+1 << " of " << element_count << std::endl;
    }

    int* p = allocator.allocate();
    std::cout << "Allocated one more block after deallocating all." << std::endl;
    allocator.deallocate(p);
    std::cout << "Deallocated the last block." << std::endl;

    try {
        int* q = allocator.allocate();
        std::cout << "This should not happen because we already allocated " << element_count+1 << " blocks." << std::endl;
    } catch (const std::exception& e) {
        std::cout << "Exception caught: " << e.what() << std::endl;
    }

    std::cout << "Demonstration complete." << std::endl;
}
```
